title: Highway Rush — 3D Endless Traffic Driving Game

requirements.summary: Create the core Highway Rush game page component with React Three Fiber scene featuring endless scrolling highway, lane markings, and third-person camera
acceptanceCriteria: Highway scrolls endlessly without visible seams or stuttering
Camera follows player car from a third-person perspective
Road displays lane markings (white dashed center lines, solid side lines)
Scene renders at smooth 60 FPS on desktop

file_operations.description: Create the main Highway Rush game component using React Three Fiber and Three.js. Implement the 3D scene with Canvas wrapper, endless highway mesh with realistic road texture and lane markings, third-person camera controller positioned behind and above the player car, and basic scene lighting (ambient and directional). Set up the game loop using useFrame hook for continuous highway scrolling and camera following. Include state management for game phase (menu, playing, paused, gameOver).

requirements.summary: Implement player car controls with keyboard (Arrow/WASD) for desktop and touch buttons for mobile, supporting smooth lane switching across at least 3 lanes
acceptanceCriteria: Arrow Left/Right and A/D keys switch lanes
Touch buttons visible and functional on mobile viewports
Lane switch is animated (smooth lateral movement)
Player car cannot move beyond the outermost lanes

file_operations.description: Add player car component as a 3D mesh with distinct color/shape. Implement lane-switching logic with keyboard event listeners for ArrowLeft/ArrowRight and A/D keys. Define at least 3 lane positions and smooth lateral interpolation between lanes using lerp or animation. Add lane boundary constraints to prevent moving beyond outermost lanes. Implement on-screen left/right touch buttons using HTML overlay elements positioned at bottom corners, visible only on mobile viewports (detected via window.innerWidth or media query). Wire touch button click handlers to the same lane-switching logic.

requirements.summary: Spawn randomized oncoming traffic cars with collision detection, increasing spawn rate and speed over time, triggering game over on collision
acceptanceCriteria: Traffic cars spawn in random lanes at random intervals
Traffic speed and spawn frequency increase over time
Collision between player and any traffic car triggers game-over state
No two traffic cars occupy the same lane at the same position simultaneously

file_operations.description: Implement traffic car spawning system that generates cars at random lanes and intervals ahead of the player. Use an array to track active traffic cars with position, lane, and color properties. Each traffic car is rendered as a simple geometric mesh (box or cylinder) with randomized colors. Implement collision detection by comparing player car bounding box with each traffic car's bounding box in the game loop. On collision, set game state to 'gameOver'. Progressively increase traffic speed and spawn frequency based on elapsed game time. Add logic to prevent overlapping traffic cars in the same lane by checking existing car positions before spawning.

requirements.summary: Implement day/night cycle with transitioning sky, lighting, and road appearance, plus motion blur post-processing at high speeds
acceptanceCriteria: Sky and lighting smoothly transition between day and night states
Motion blur effect activates and intensifies as speed increases
Day/night cycle repeats on a set interval during gameplay

file_operations.description: Add day/night cycle system that interpolates sky color (background) and ambient light intensity over a fixed time interval (e.g., 60 seconds). Use a time counter in the game loop to calculate cycle phase (0-1) and apply smooth color transitions. Update directional light color and intensity to reflect day (bright) vs night (dim) states. Apply React Three Fiber EffectComposer with UnrealBloomPass and a custom motion blur pass that activates and intensifies when speed exceeds a threshold. Store current cycle phase in state and ensure it loops continuously during gameplay.

requirements.summary: Trigger particle explosion, screen shake, and slow-motion effects on collision; award bonus points and slow-motion on near-misses
acceptanceCriteria: Crash triggers particle burst and screen shake
Slow-motion effect activates on crash and on near-misses
Near-miss bonus points are added to the score and displayed briefly
Effects do not permanently alter game state

file_operations.description: Implement particle system for crash effects by spawning multiple small meshes at collision point with random velocities, fading them out over time. Add screen shake by temporarily offsetting camera position with random jitter, decaying over a short duration. Implement slow-motion by temporarily reducing game speed multiplier (e.g., to 0.3x) for 1-2 seconds on crash or near-miss. Define near-miss threshold distance and check each frame if any traffic car passes within that distance without colliding; if so, award bonus points (e.g., +50) and display a temporary 'NEAR MISS!' message overlay. Ensure effects reset after their duration without permanently altering game state.

requirements.summary: Display HUD overlay with live score, high score, speed meter, and coin count; persist high score to localStorage on game over
acceptanceCriteria: HUD shows live score, high score, speed, and coin count
High score is loaded from and saved to localStorage on game over
Speed meter reflects current in-game speed value
HUD is readable and non-intrusive over the 3D scene

file_operations.description: Add HUD overlay component rendered as HTML div positioned absolutely over the 3D canvas. Display current score (incremented each frame based on distance traveled), high score (loaded from localStorage key 'highwayRush_highScore' on mount), current speed value, and coin count. Style HUD with neon-themed text and semi-transparent background for readability. On game over, compare current score with high score and update localStorage if new high score is achieved. Ensure HUD updates reactively via state changes.

requirements.summary: Spawn collectible coins on the highway and implement a car skins system with at least 3 unlockable skins persisted in localStorage
acceptanceCriteria: Coins spawn on the road and are collected on overlap with player car
Coin count persists across sessions via localStorage
At least 3 car skins available, each with a distinct color/style
Skins can be unlocked and selected from the main menu or a skins screen
Selected skin is applied to the player car in-game

file_operations.description: Add coin spawning system that places collectible coin meshes (golden cylinders or toruses) at random lane positions ahead of the player. Check for overlap between player car and coins each frame; on collection, increment coin count and remove coin from scene. Persist coin count to localStorage key 'highwayRush_coins'. Define at least 3 car skins (e.g., default blue, red racer, neon green) with distinct colors or materials. In the main menu screen, add a Car Skins section that displays available skins with unlock prices. Allow player to purchase skins by spending coins and save purchased/selected skin to localStorage key 'highwayRush_selectedSkin'. On game start, load selected skin from localStorage and apply its material/color to the player car mesh.

requirements.summary: Escalate difficulty every 30 seconds by increasing traffic density, speed cap, and spawn variety, with on-screen notification
acceptanceCriteria: Difficulty level increments every 30 seconds of gameplay
Each difficulty increment increases traffic density and max speed
An on-screen notification briefly appears on difficulty increase

file_operations.description: Implement difficulty scaling system that increments difficulty level every 30 seconds of active gameplay (tracked via elapsed time counter). On each difficulty increase, raise traffic spawn frequency multiplier, increase max traffic speed cap, and optionally increase spawn variety (e.g., faster cars, more lanes occupied). Display a brief notification overlay (e.g., 'DIFFICULTY INCREASED!' in neon text) that fades out after 2 seconds. Store current difficulty level in state and use it to modulate spawn parameters.

requirements.summary: Implement all game screens: Main Menu, Pause overlay, and Game Over screen with ad placeholder zones
acceptanceCriteria: Main menu renders before game start with Play and Skins options
Pause overlay halts game loop and shows Resume/Restart
Game over screen shows score, high score, coins, restart and menu buttons
Rewarded ad placeholder button visible on game over screen
Interstitial ad placeholder zone shown after game over dismissal
Banner ad placeholder visible at the bottom of the main menu and game over screens

file_operations.description: Add Main Menu screen component that renders before game start, displaying game title, Play button, Car Skins button, and Leaderboard/High Score section. Include a banner ad placeholder div at the bottom. Add Pause overlay component that appears when Escape key is pressed during gameplay, pausing the game loop (stop useFrame updates) and showing Resume and Restart buttons. Add Game Over screen component that displays final score, high score, coins collected this run, Restart and Main Menu buttons. Include a rewarded ad placeholder button labeled 'Watch Ad for Extra Life' and a banner ad placeholder at the bottom. On dismissing game over (via Restart or Main Menu), briefly show an interstitial ad placeholder overlay before transitioning. Use conditional rendering based on game state (menu, playing, paused, gameOver).

requirements.summary: Implement all audio using Web Audio API: engine sound, crash sound, background music, and button click sounds, with mute toggle persisted in localStorage
acceptanceCriteria: Engine sound loops and pitch-shifts based on current speed
Crash sound plays on collision
Background music loops throughout gameplay
Button click sound plays on all interactive buttons
Mute toggle button in HUD silences all audio
Mute state persists via localStorage

file_operations.description: Create a custom React hook that provides procedural audio using Web Audio API. Generate and control: a looping engine sound (oscillator with pitch modulation based on current speed), a crash/explosion sound (noise burst with decay envelope), a looping background music track (synthesized melody using multiple oscillators), and button click sounds (short beep). Expose play/stop functions for each sound and a global mute toggle. Load mute state from localStorage key 'highwayRush_muted' on mount and persist changes. Ensure all audio nodes are properly connected to AudioContext and cleaned up on unmount.

Import and use the useHighwayRushSound hook. Start engine sound and background music on game start, stop them on game over. Modulate engine sound pitch in the game loop based on current speed. Trigger crash sound on collision. Trigger button click sound on all button interactions (Play, Restart, Pause, Resume, etc.). Add a mute toggle button to the HUD that calls the hook's mute toggle function and updates localStorage.

requirements.summary: Register Highway Rush on the Home page as a game card and add the /highway-rush route to the router
acceptanceCriteria: Highway Rush appears as a card on the Home page
Clicking the card navigates to /highway-rush
The game component loads correctly at that route

file_operations.description: Add a new game card for Highway Rush in the arcade games section. Use a car/racing icon (e.g., 🏎️ or Car from lucide-react) and description 'Dodge traffic at high speeds in this 3D endless runner'. Set the card's link to navigate to '/highway-rush' using TanStack Router Link component.

Import HighwayRushGame component lazily and add a new route configuration for path '/highway-rush' that renders the HighwayRushGame component. Ensure the route is wrapped in the same Suspense boundary as other game routes.

requirements.summary: Integrate Highway Rush with the existing player profile and XP system, awarding XP based on distance score and recording stats, plus milestone achievements
acceptanceCriteria: XP is awarded at game over proportional to score
Games played counter increments each run
Milestone score achievements unlock and notify via existing achievement system

file_operations.description: Import playerProfile utilities (awardXP, trackWin, trackPlay) and achievements utilities (unlockAchievement). On game over, call trackPlay to increment games played. Award XP proportional to final score (e.g., score / 10). Check if final score reaches milestone thresholds (e.g., 1000, 5000, 10000) and unlock corresponding achievements (define new achievement IDs in achievements.ts if needed, such as 'highway_1000', 'highway_5000', 'highway_10000'). Ensure achievements trigger notifications via the existing achievement system.

Add new achievement definitions for Highway Rush milestones: 'highway_1000' (Reach 1000 points in Highway Rush), 'highway_5000' (Reach 5000 points), and 'highway_10000' (Reach 10000 points). Assign appropriate categories ('arcade' or new 'highway' category), points, and descriptions.
